heading: Why does my cluster have multiple connection strings?
body:
To connect to Atlas, point your applications to a URI (Uniform Resource Identifier) to
communicate with a cluster. Atlas creates clusters with more than
one node or host. Each node has its own hostname that resolves to an IP
address. The URI (Uniform Resource Identifier), known as a *connection string*, to which Atlas
connects might have more than one hostname. Configure Atlas to
accept connections to the cluster hosts from allowed IP addresses.

Atlas secures connections from public IP address through
authentication and TLS (Transport Layer Security). If you want to connect to private IP
addresses, you can use:

- AWS and GCP VPC Peering

- Azure VNet Peering

- [Private Endpoint-Aware Connection Strings](private-endpoint-connection-strings)

These features all manage communication over internal IP addresses
within secure networks.

Atlas provides more than one connection string when using secure
networks. Each network offers a string that resolves to different IP
addresses.

All clusters have a
[standard connection string](connstring-standard). This resolves
to the cluster's:

- Public IP addresses for Internet connections and

- VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) private IP addresses for AWS (Amazon Web Services) clusters when resolved from a
  peered VPC (Virtual Private Cloud).

Use this string for applications connecting over the Internet or
connecting to peered clusters in AWS (Amazon Web Services).

Clusters with peered networks have a
[Private IP for Peering connection strings](connstring-private).
This string resolves to IP addresses available to:

- Peered networks in Azure (Microsoft Azure) or Google Cloud

- AWS (Amazon Web Services) peered clusters with a custom DNS (Domain Name System) service.

Use this connection string with applications connecting:

- Within an Azure (Microsoft Azure) or Google Cloud peered network

- To AWS (Amazon Web Services) clusters when using AWS (Amazon Web Services) with custom DNS (Domain Name System) service.

AWS (Amazon Web Services) or Azure (Microsoft Azure) clusters in regions with [Private Endpoint-Aware Connection Strings](private-endpoint-connection-strings) configured have one or more
connection strings. Each string resolves to the private IP address of a
network interface in your VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) or VNet that connects directly to a load
balancer in the Atlas VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) or VNet. Use these connection strings
with applications connecting with private endpoints.
